title: Building Engineer
description:
About the role:

As a Engineer, you will be responsible for implementing, coordinating, and managing all mechanical and electrical operations for a facility, office building, data center. This job is part of the Engineering and Technical Services job function. They are responsible for providing support, preventive maintenance, and repairs on equipment and systems.

What you'll do:

1. Planned Preventive Maintenance & Reactive Maintenance

  • Review and monitor 52 PPM schedules.
  • Planning, scheduling of equipment wise PPM's in 52-week planner and integrate with REALM tool.
  • Closure of work orders with proper service records as per SLA.
  • Be ready for sample audits on monthly basis. This includes all activities are updated 100% on REALMS.
  • Maintain complete information and compliance to O&M standard on service record for Clients assets where applicable e.g. WO number, no task undone, no blank field, review by Engineer.
  • POC/PIC for all sites electrical power shutdown activities.
  • POC/PIC for any site incident

2. Asset Verification

Tagging of Engineering assets and its physical verification on periodic basis. (Identification of asset (stock take) thru PPM and update in REALM as and when change required in the registry.)

3. Compliance to Operation & Maintenance (O&M) Standards

  • Adherence to O&M standards.
  • Prepare normal and emergency change requests review on weekly basis which includes close coordination and approvals from various business heads so that the risks are mitigated, and task is performed without incidents.
  • Client change request review weekly
  • Ensure works are performed safety utilizing CBRE Control of Works procedure.

4. Incident Management and Reporting

  • Incident reporting to all stake holders, raising BIR, problem tickets and meeting timelines to resolve.
  • Through investigations, RCA 's and mitigation plans to avoid such similar incidents across country sites.
  • Evaluate the root cause, prepare a plan to mitigate risks and implement control measures that similar incidents do not occur in other locations.
  • WMS preparation and review in detail so that incidents do not occur.
  • Collaborate with
  • Client on preparation and execution as needed for CAP for medium and high risks.
  • Working Protocols

5. Lifecycle planning and Budgeting (CIP)

  • Tracking engineering equipment’s life cycle asper O&M, OEM guidelines for planning and budgeting of replacement.
  • Periodic review of assets and place them in CIP projects.

What You’ll Need:

  • High School Diploma or GED with up to 4 years of job-related experience.
  • Experienced in interpreting electrical diagrams and ACMV Troubleshooting
  • Experienced in executing power shutdowns.
  • Experience in the areas of staffing, selection, training, development, coaching, mentoring, measuring, appraising, and rewarding performance and retention preferred.
  • Leadership skills to motivate team impact on quality, efficiency, and effectiveness of the job discipline and department.
  • Excellent client relationship skills.
  • Communication skills to exchange straightforward information.
  • Working knowledge of Microsoft Office products. Examples include Word, Excel, Outlook, etc.
  • Strong organizational skills with an inquisitive mindset.
  • 24/7 standby
  • Require commitment to work after office hours to solve engineering issues at site.

postedCompany.description:
About CBRE

With broader and deeper capabilities than any other company, CBRE is the leading full-service real estate services and investment organization in the world.

CBRE Group, Inc. is the world’s largest commercial real estate services and investment firm, with 2016 revenues of $13.1 billion and more than 75,000 employees (excluding affiliate offices). CBRE has been included in the Fortune 500 since 2008, ranking #214 in 2017. It also has been voted the industry’s top brand by the Lipsey Company for 16 consecutive years, and has been named one of Fortune’s “Most Admired Companies” in the real estate sector for six years in a row.  Its shares trade on the New York Stock Exchange under the symbol “CBG.”

CBRE offers a broad range of integrated services, including facilities, transaction and project management; property management; investment management; appraisal and valuation; property leasing; strategic consulting; property sales; mortgage services and development services.
